original_poem:
गांधी मला भेटला  
सप्तरंगाच्या इंद्रधनुष्याच्या  
एकेका कणात तो होता,  
स्वप्नांच्या त्या सरितेच्या  
काठावर तो उभा होता.
translated_poem:
I met Gandhi  
In every grain  
Of the seven-colored rainbow,  
He stood  
On the shore  
Of that river of dreams.

preprocessing.alignment_note: Source and translation stanza counts match.

emotional_arc.value: A quiet, wonder-filled encounter with Gandhi's presence diffused across a rainbow and a dreamlike riverbank, moving from intimate meeting to visionary stillness.

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: Gandhi's ideals dispersed into every fragment of the visible world's spectrum
the threshold between lived reality and the nation's unrealized aspirations
the continuous flow of ideals and visionary thoughts
omnipresence within hope and natural beauty

visual_motifs.motif: a seven-colored rainbow whose every grain holds a human figure
a solitary figure standing on the bank of a river of dreams
figure standing on the shore of a river
Gandhi in every grain of a seven-colored rainbow
Gandhi standing on the shore of a river of dreams

parsed.emotional_arc: The speaker experiences a spiritual realization of Gandhi's omnipresence within nature and the realm of ideals.

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: The omnipresence of Gandhi's philosophy within the diverse beauty of the world.
The continuous flow of idealistic aspirations and the subconscious search for truth.

visual_motifs: A figure standing on a riverbank

cultural_entities.translation_note: Mahatma Gandhi is invoked here not just as a historical figure but as a 'Mahatma' (Great Soul), a transcendent spiritual guide.
